channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Maryland senator is returning to US after pushing for Abrego Garcia's release and meeting with him
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen is flying home from El Salvador after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump turns a COVID information website into a promotion page for the lab leak theory
item.description: A federal website that used to feature information on vaccines, testing and treatment for COVID-19 has been transformed into a page supporting the theory that the pandemic originated with a lab leak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dan Patrick endorses Texas House’s voucher plan, clearing path to final approval
item.description: The lieutenant governor proposes that the Texas Senate adopt the House bill, which would eliminate a negotiation over the two versions and cement the victory for supporters.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison And Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Dr. Oz young family member faints during Trump's remarks at White House swearing-in ceremony
item.description: A young girl collapsed near the Resolute Desk in the Oval Office while President Donald Trump spoke during a Friday swearing-in ceremony for former heart surgeon Dr. Mehmet Oz, who rushed over to assist the child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge won’t take further steps to enforce his order in AP case against Trump administration
item.description: A federal judge who ordered the Trump administration to stop blocking The Associated Press’ from presidential events has refused to take immediate steps to get White House officials to comply.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China's military pressure near Taiwan is up 300%: What it means for the US
item.description: Paparo said the U.S can no longer sit back amid “unprecedented aggression and military modernization" by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: Digging into Minnesota‘s political history
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and a team of MPR News reporters and producers share a bit Minnesota political history with some modern twists.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Top GOP lawmaker, Hochul trade barbs amid speculation Trump ally is jumping in gubernatorial race
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul and Rep. Elise Stefanik, R-N.Y., have already begun trading barbs in the 2026 gubernatorial race amid reports that the Republican congresswoman is considering a run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge pauses Trump administration's plans for mass layoffs at Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: President Donald Trump’s attempt to fire nearly everyone at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has been paused by a federal judge, who says she's “deeply concerned” about the plan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harvard records requested by Education Department
item.description: The records request comes after the Trump administration has made multiple moves against the Ivy League.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge bucks Trump admin, delays dismissal of of MS-13 leader's case
item.description: A federal judge blocked the Trump administration from dropping charges against alleged MS-13 leader Henrry Villatoro Santos, ordering he remain in U.S. custody for now.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls Sen. Van Hollen a 'fool' after meeting with deported MS-13 suspect in El Salvador
item.description: President Donald Trump is calling Sen. Chris Van Hollen a "fool" after the Maryland Democrat flew to El Salvador to meet with Kilmar Abrego Garcia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Radical activist who fueled Cori Bush's campaigns emerges as spokesperson for track star's murder suspect
item.description: A spokesperson for the family of the 17-year-old boy accused of stabbing a teen track star to death has a long history of supporting defunding the police as well as close ties to a former 'Squad' Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: President Donald Trump says negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insists that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Draft budget plan proposes deep cuts across federal health programs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A draft budget proposal circulating among federal officials would dramatically deepen cuts at the nation's top health agency, eliminating some public health programs entirely and serving as a roadmap for more mass firings. The document suggests a cut in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghanistan Medal of Honor recipient reenlists in Marine Corps reserves after 15 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dakota Meyer, a Marine who was awarded the Medal of Honor for heroism in the Afghanistan War but later became a sharp critic of the Biden administration over its chaotic withdrawal from that conflict, has reenlisted in the military and will serve in the Marine Reserves.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: At contentious Wisconsin elections meeting, Democratic chair threatens to oust Republican official
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin Elections Commission discussion of uncounted ballots in Madison devolved into a shouting match Thursday, with the Democratic chair threatening to kick a Republican commissioner out of the meeting.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Property tax funding for 825 Las Vegas police officers on track at Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers in Carson City voted Wednesday to continue the "More Cops" tax that was put in place 30 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Supervisor Lawson-Remer presents 'local battleplan' during State of County address
item.description: This year’s state of the county address was delivered in Balboa Park by acting Chair Terra Lawson-Remer. She shared her proposed plan for how to move the county forward in challenging fiscal times and what she considers “a moment of democratic crisis.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: IRS asked to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: Only the IRS can review and revoke the existing status of taxpaying entities and has traditionally done so without presidential interference, as mandated by tax laws.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's wife sought restraining order in 2021, DHS says
item.description: The wife of Kilmar Abrego Garcia filed for an order of protection against her husband in 2021, saying he was violent toward her.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: PIX on Politics Daily: NYC mayoral election poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Welcome to PIX on Politics Daily with Dan Mannarino, where we break down the day's political news, headlines, and issues that matter most to you through in-depth conversation. Join us daily on PIX11+ streaming at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Keri Heintzeman wins Republican primary in race to fill Justin Eichorn’s state Senate seat
item.description: The winner of the April 29 special election will fill the District 6 seat left vacant by Justin Eichorn, who resigned last month after he was charged with soliciting a minor.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: NIL payments OK at UNLV, UNR, but payment amounts secret under SB293
item.description: A bill concerning NIL — name, image, likeness — and how UNLV and UNR can manage contracts directly with players passed on Tuesday in the Nevada Senate, advancing to the Assembly with other bills. A Republican leader objected to secrecy surrounding the payment amounts.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: States push Medicaid work rules, but few programs help enrollees find jobs
item.description: Some lawmakers are pushing to require that Medicaid recipients work in order to get or keep coverage, and some states already try to help them find jobs. But the effects of those efforts are unclear.
item.dc:creator: Sam Whitehead, Phil Galewitz, and Katheryn Houghton, MPR News

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Getting the IRS on the phone is more difficult this tax filing season, experts say
item.description: Taxpayers calling the IRS for help processing their taxes this filing season may find it harder than normal to get someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
